author: @Steven-wm4bt
text: Goodbye Robert, you will always be remembered. Thank you for so many years

author: @JohnWalsh-p6v
text: To create decent rollers cheaply use pvc pipe.. thin pipe is sold at bnq... use pva mixed with mica to coat the positively charged roller and straight pvc pipe for the negative roller... works well with neoprene as a band... i use my vandergraaf with the negative connected to a capacitor array(200kv) anode and then to earth through a small tesla coil secondary with the vandergraaf output charging the positive of my electrostatic capacitor array, which discharges through a spark gap and then into the tesla coil primary and back into ground... this creates an increased magnetic field strength in the coil pulling more negative onto the anode which increases the charge onto the cathode... the whole thing increases charge rate... if a high enough voltage can be attained it can be converted back down through transformers and resistors to produce a constant flow of energy to be used at more mainstream voltages... iv enhanced the design further with 3 vandergraafs charging 3 seperate capacitor array cathodes and 3 seperate spark gap cathodes that all discharge into 1 spark gap anode... the 3 cathodes aligned triangular with the anode central... i call it the fluxed zerpoint capacitor array... i run my vandergraaf motor from solar and battery but am currently working on improving the belt system to enable the system to be used continually... using antenna the energy can also be transmitted over greater distances when utilized wirelessly... all you really need are 2 rollers made of opposing tribeo electric charges pressed against each other on rotating axels one roller attached to a motor and conductive antenna... you dont need a dome... attach instead to a capacitor cathode... make multiple static capacitors of equal dimensions and connect in series to increase the charge... doing this will increase the amount of joules produced which is not only reflected in the length of the sparks produced but also in their thickness indicating increased strength over a thinner stream of hv plasma discharge... this is how wardencliffe worked just without the vandergraaf the capacitor positive connected to the dome and negative to earth through the tesla coil... let me assure you, the static produced off the coil is phenominal and to think hardly any power is needed to run such a device... all you need for more power is a wider band and antenna. If built appropriately a handheld vandergraaf could produce a million volts with a backpack producing multiples of millions... we could charge ourselves up negatively and float over the ground or make special conductive roads that charge up from the produced field of the backpack and allow us to fly around... via repulsion... weeeeee3e

replies.author: @Anita-w7n6i
replies.text:
Hello, that sounds really interesting. I'm looking for an emergency device to run my mixer. What must I learn to do that? Yesterday I came across this channel and had liked to become a member. Robert was a great man and a great teacher. May he rest in peace. Wonderful work! AI gives us opportunities that had been impossible 3 years ago. ChatGPT taught me ODEs before it could compute correctly, but it ever had the right hints, tools, syntax to solve the problems. I have founded an educational initiative and want to help young people to reach their desired jobs; normally a duty of our government that should represent people (Germany, Bonner Grundgesetz of 1949). 
This year is a "practice year" for our initiative together with a nearby makerspace - and it would be great if we could transform this voltage into a working current. I know that sounds ridiculous, but now are things possible that had been impossible before. I think free energy is already there - but in the wrong hands.

author: @JohnWalsh-p6v
text: Should build a cylindrical housing and wrap foil around it to create a larger surface area instead of a dome... i think the ferromagnetic properties of the steel do help with field strength so id further recomend coating with conductive polyethylene based resin... mix with graphite powder and silver oxide coat the exterior and then dust exterior with fine iron powder. Graphite has high conductivity and quite low resistance, silver has less resistance than graphite which should enhance the conductivity while the iron coating when exposed to a static charge will allow for increased field strength... aluminium from what i have seen works better in most cases as a anode... steel, copper, silver and gaphite all align as a cathode when used with aluminium
